← the Atlas

Remote terminals — the finale

budding·proposed·JUN 30, 2026

The plan of record for the last leg of kolu#951 — kolu dials remote hosts. R8's reader-join stays; the awareness architecture is settled (split observing from remembering — a memoryless producer per host emits TerminalSnapshot events, kolu alone folds them and owns the memory). What changed is the SEQUENCE. The old three-parallel-prep split (PR-1 lifecycle · PR-2 fs/git · PR-3 awareness → one F-REMOTE feature) is replaced. The remaining work is now: (1) extract the terminal-workflow SUBSTRATE as a @kolu/* package both UIs embed — sealed id-keyed dispatch, host registry, reconnect/adoption, composite TerminalKey — which isolates the host-lifecycle volatility (about half of kolu-server) and makes "hard-pin local" a compile error; (2) kolu-tui, the second consumer that EMBEDS the substrate and proves it remote-first; (3) full-client F-REMOTE, the whole web tile on the proven substrate; (4) R10 canvas. Phases — S1·S2·R9.0·R9.1 shipped · SUBSTRATE · KOLU-TUI · F-REMOTE · R10.

Contents

  • Roadmap
  • What it reuses — and what it must build
  • The shipped foundation
    • S1 · S2 — the awareness foundation (shipped )
    • R9.0 — kolu runs the producer in-process, folds (shipped )
    • R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver (shipped )
  • The remaining phases
    • SUBSTRATE — extract @kolu/sutra (do next · the spine)
    • KOLU-TUI — the second consumer, remote-first (the de-risk milestone)
    • F-REMOTE — the complete web tile on the proven substrate (nothing deferred)
    • R10 — canvas multiplex + validate
  • Open decisions

The last leg of #951 — kolu dials remote hosts. R8 (#1594) shipped the right reader-join (a terminal is kolu.authored ⋈ terminalWorkspace.snapshots, joined at the reader) — keep that. The awareness architecture for a remote host is settled — split observing from remembering: a memoryless producer runs beside each host’s kaval and emits TerminalSnapshot events; kolu alone folds them, owns the memory (on kolu.authored), and stamps recency with its own clock (the awareness design). What this note re-settles is the sequence: the remaining work is re-cut around extracting the terminal-workflow substrate — the receptacle that isolates the host-lifecycle volatility — with kolu-tui as the second consumer that proves it remote-first, then the full web tile, then the canvas.

The settled design — and the two directions it replaced

The awareness boundary is observe vs. remember (the awareness design, outcome of a grounded 3-agent debate). It supersedes two earlier attempts, both dead: (1) the local-pulam-PROCESS (#1614, CLOSED) — conflated a process boundary with code decoupling and introduced a fold-clobber intrinsic to mirroring an ephemeral process; (2) createPulam / “share the assembly” — shared the wrong thing (the assembly still fused deriving with storing). The converged fix deletes the assembly: the producer is memoryless (it can’t even spell memory, by type), so there’s nothing to clobber. R9·lib therefore dissolved.

What changed in this re-plan — the prep-split is dead, the substrate is the spine

The previous version of this note sequenced three parallel local-preserving prep PRs (PR-1 lifecycle · PR-2 fs/git · PR-3 awareness) → one complete-everything F-REMOTE → R10. That split is replaced. The round-2 type-enforcement debate (debates/pr1637/) showed the lifecycle prep’s real problem: routing is a convention, not a boundary — the local endpoint is importable, so “hard-pin local” is a writable expression a missed seam forgets. The fix isn’t to thread the seam by hand (PR-1); it’s to lift the whole host-lifecycle volatility into a sealed @kolu/* package both UIs embed — the terminal substrate. That makes the seam-hunt a compile error, slims kolu-server by ~half, and turns ssh-remote into an additive endpoint. So PR-1 dissolves into the substrate; PR-2 (fs/git) and PR-3 (awareness rendering) reframe from “preps that gate remote” into full-client surface work that lands in F-REMOTE; and kolu-tui is inserted as the second consumer that proves the substrate remote-first. (Strategic debate + the lone dissent: debates/pr1637/r3.*.)

Roadmap

FINALE — substrate → kolu-tui → full-client F-REMOTE → R10✓ shipped · ▶ do next · ◐ build-clean · ○ todo · → drill in
✓S1 · memoryless awareness producer — emits observations, no seed, TerminalSnapshot onlyshipped #1626 · → awareness
✓S2 · kolu's fold owns memory — typed patches · kolu's clock · memory→authored · event streamshipped #1626
✓R9.0 · kolu runs the producer in-process, folds onto authored + snapshotsshipped #1626
✓R9.1 · the TerminalEndpoint resolver (the one HostLocation-keyed seam)shipped #1603
▶SUBSTRATE · extract @kolu/sutra — sealed id-keyed dispatch · private endpoints · host registry · forEachHost · reconnect/adoption · composite TerminalKeyneeds R9.1 · do next
○KOLU-TUI · the second consumer, remote-first — embeds the substrate, dials hosts, proves the host-lifecycle volatility on ~⅓ the surfaceneeds SUBSTRATE
○F-REMOTE · the complete web tile on the proven substrate — PTY · fs/git Code tab · byte preview · full awareness · restore · sleep/wake · paste/upload · transcript (nothing deferred)needs KOLU-TUI · R7
○R10 · canvas multiplex + host picker + validateneeds F-REMOTE

S1 + S2 + R9.0 + R9.1 shipped (#1626, #1603) — the memoryless producer, kolu’s fold, the in-process local awareness, and the HostLocation resolver. The remaining four phases run mostly in sequence (each builds on the last): the substrate is the foundation; kolu-tui proves it remote-first; F-REMOTE lands the complete web tile on top; R10 is the picker/multiplex UX. The old PR-1/PR-2/PR-3 prep rows are gone — see the re-plan callout above.

What it reuses — and what it must build

Shipped seams (reuse as-is):

seam where leg it serves
the per-terminal sensors → memoryless producer @kolu/terminal-workspace the observation stream (git · pr · agent · foreground), local or remote
the pulam daemon pulam (R4) the one producer per host — serves awareness and fs/git on one surface
getHostSession @kolu/surface-nix-host a pooled, long-lived ssh/Nix dial, keyed (host, binary) — kaval for PTY, pulam for awareness+fs/git
frontDaemonOverStdio · kaval --stdio @kolu/surface-daemon · kaval dial a remote kaval over ssh stdio → a durable remote PTY that outlives the link
mirrorRemoteSurface @kolu/surface the total dual ({ procedures, done }, R7) — mirror a remote producer’s stream into one local handle
HostLocation remote arm · persisted location @kolu/common { kind:"remote", hostId }, round-trips to disk
the reader-join (composeTerminalMetadata) @kolu/common · client authored ⋈ snapshots at read + at disk — unchanged
the TerminalEndpoint resolver kolu-server (R9.1) the one HostLocation-keyed seam the substrate seals and makes private

Must build:

gap today the phase
lifecycle / registry / reconnect / adoption as a sealed boundary, not a convention smeared across kolu-server the local endpoint is importable → “hard-pin local” is a writable expression; one ptyHostClient singleton SUBSTRATE
a second consumer that embeds the substrate and dials hosts itself — the proof it graduated only the web client consumes the lifecycle, in-process KOLU-TUI
the complete web remote tile — fs/git Code tab + byte preview + rich awareness + restore + paste/upload + transcript + the create knob only {kind:"local"} exists; the Code tab rides value-streams; the preview byte route reads kolu-server’s local fs F-REMOTE
host picker + canvas multiplex one local kaval, no picker R10

The shipped foundation

S1 · S2 — the awareness foundation (shipped #1626)

S1· memoryless awareness producer — observations, no seed, TerminalSnapshot only✓ shipped
needs ← R6 · R4
S2· kolu's fold owns memory — kolu's clock, memory→authored✓ shipped
needs ← S1

S1 rewrote the sensors into a memoryless producer: per-field TerminalEvent events, no durable memory, no seed; its export type TerminalSnapshot (the five re-observable fields) cannot spell the memory fields. S2 gave kolu the only fold: fold(cur, o, ctx) → TerminalState ({snapshot, memory}), last-write-wins for the five snapshot fields, stamping recency on a live agent-identity change with kolu’s clock, remembering lastAgentCommand. Memory lands on kolu.authored — so every snapshots collection everywhere is TerminalSnapshot. The full types/API/flows are in the awareness design.

R9.0 — kolu runs the producer in-process, folds (shipped #1626)

R9.0· kolu runs the memoryless producer in-process and folds✓ shipped
needs ← S2links #1626

kolu’s local awareness is the memoryless producer called in-process, its observations fed straight into kolu’s fold (memory→kolu.authored, the rest→snapshots). Clobber-free by type. A 4-reviewer foundation debate then assessed it transport-agnostic — the same fold and producer serve the remote case verbatim (debates/r93-foundation/).

R9.1 — the TerminalEndpoint resolver (shipped #1603)

R9.1· HostLocation-keyed endpoint resolver✓ shipped
needs ← R8links #1603

resolveTerminalEndpoint is the one HostLocation-keyed seam mapping a terminal to its kaval endpoint; the {kind:"remote"} arm fails loudly (an .exhaustive() match) until the remote endpoint fills it. This is exactly the seam the substrate makes private and sealed — R9.1 built the resolver; the substrate hides it so it can’t be bypassed.

The remaining phases

SUBSTRATE — extract @kolu/sutra (do next · the spine)

SUBSTRATE· extract the terminal-workflow substrate — sealed dispatch · private endpoints · host registry · reconnect/adoption · composite TerminalKey▶ do next
needs ← R9.1blocks → KOLU-TUI · F-REMOTElinks the substrate note

Lift the host-lifecycle volatility into one @kolu/* package both UIs embed. Per-terminal ops become op(key) that resolve the endpoint internally; the endpoint, the resolver and the nine *Local* helpers go package-private, so “hard-pin local / bypass the host router” becomes a compile error (no symbol to import). create(location) is the sole location-supply; session-global ops go through forEachHost + a branded complete-host sweep. Terminal identity becomes the composite {host, ptyId} TerminalKey (the wire stays bare; meta.location becomes a derived projection). This absorbs the manual lifecycle-threading prep (the seam-hunt is now the compiler’s job) and migrates ~half of kolu-server out of the web server.

  • Why whole: a pure local-preserving refactor + package move — every record is {kind:"local"} today, so every op(key) resolves to the same local endpoint; create/attach/kill/sleep/wake/adoption stay byte-identical. No user-facing change.
  • The full design — API spec, the compile-error guarantee, the composite key + staged migration, what moves vs stays — is in the substrate note.
  • Needs: R9.1. Blocks: kolu-tui and the full client both embed the result.
  • Done: the sealed TerminalSubstrate facade ships; the endpoint/resolver/*Local* helpers are package-private (importing one outside the package fails to compile); kolu-server drives every op through the facade; TerminalKey is the registry key with meta.location derived; all terminal e2e + sleepWake.test.ts + boot-adoption pass unchanged; a mixed-host reconcile test + the create-location reject test land.

KOLU-TUI — the second consumer, remote-first (the de-risk milestone)

KOLU-TUI· kolu-tui embeds the substrate and proves it remote-first — local+remote PTY · reconnect · boot re-adopt · adoption · basic awareness○ todo
needs ← SUBSTRATEblocks → F-REMOTElinks the kolu-tui note

kolu itself, in the terminal — it embeds the substrate (Option B — own process, own state, not a thin client of a running kolu-server) and dials hosts itself, rendering kolu’s own two-pane layout — the Dock rail you switch from, the live terminal you type into — as a full-screen TUI on OpenTUI that reuses the shared agentProjection folds, across local and ssh hosts, with no Code browser. It is the second consumer that proves the substrate graduated — and it MUST exercise the pulam dial for awareness, which (because awareness and fs/git ride the same getHostSession({binary:"pulam"}) surface) proves fs/git’s transport for free. It ships local+remote PTY + reconnect + boot re-dial-re-adopt + adoption + basic awareness — the hardest, least-reversible third of remote terminals — and is a usable product. It deliberately skips the Code tab, byte preview, paste/upload and transcript.

  • Why it’s the proof, not just a product: kaval-tui already proves raw PTY transport — kolu-tui’s added value is the kolu orchestration both consumers share, plus the dial coverage. The two conditions (embed + exercise the pulam dial) are what keep it a de-risk rather than a second UI.
  • The surface, the UX prototypes, the phases (K1 embed + the Dock · K2 survive-the-link · K3 live Dock badges over the pulam dial) are in the kolu-tui note.
  • Needs: SUBSTRATE. Blocks: F-REMOTE inherits a proven substrate.
  • Done: a remote pane is created/attached/driven/killed through the embedded substrate; the link can drop and recover; kolu-tui restart re-dials + re-adopts; two hosts’ colliding bare ids stay distinct via TerminalKey; a remote agent’s working→awaiting shows on the board over the pulam dial.

F-REMOTE — the complete web tile on the proven substrate (nothing deferred)

F-REMOTE· remote terminal — PTY · fs/git Code tab · byte preview · full awareness · restore · sleep/wake · paste/upload · transcript○ todo
needs ← KOLU-TUI · R7blocks → R10

The web/electron remote tile, lit whole on the substrate kolu-tui already proved. The hard constraint stands: a remote tile is as complete as a local tile the moment a user can create one — nothing deferred. This is where the fs/git Code-tab procedure+pulse, the byte primitives, and the awareness framer’s consumer rendering + restore UX land — no longer preps that gate remote, but the web surface on a proven foundation. A remote tile becomes a full peer to a local one:

  • PTY over the remote kaval (the substrate’s remote endpoint; remote spawn-env from the host’s system.info).

  • Real fs/git Code tab + the host-aware binary-preview byte route over the remote pulam (R7 mirror) — the transport kolu-tui already exercised; F-REMOTE adds the rendering: rewrite the Code tab off koluSurface’s value-streams onto the shared surface’s procedure + {seq} pulse, add the byte primitives (range-capable preview-read, scratch-write, transcript-read), re-enforce the realpath/traversal guard on the producing host.

  • Full awareness — subscribe terminalEvents on the same mirror, fold via the same fold, keyed by the substrate’s composite TerminalKey. Agent badge + recency + restore-target, richly rendered.

  • Session restore incl. boot re-dial-re-adopt — reuses the substrate’s forEachHost boot loop (proven by kolu-tui); the client restore loop dials+adopts, not respawns.

  • kill/attach (substrate-routed); sleep/wake on the host; paste/upload to the host; transcript from the host.

  • The create knob (“On SSH host…” → location:{kind:"remote",hostId}, the substrate’s create(location)), the ssh badge + a discoverability tip, the remote-hostId daemonStatus writer, and the Nix wiring.

  • Why whole: a remote tile matches a local tile on every axis — and every substrate primitive it rides was proven by kolu-tui, so the only new risk is the web rendering, not the transport. All local e2e stay green.

  • Needs: KOLU-TUI · R7. Not R-pulamweb-4; not any further contract bump beyond the byte primitives.

  • Done: an e2e creates a remote tile and asserts EVERY local-tile axis — PTY echo; Code-tab git/diff/tree + binary preview over real remote fs/git; live agent/recency/restore badges folded via the same fold; kill/attach; sleep/wake, paste/upload, transcript on the host; kolu-restart re-adopt; reload re-dial+adopt. All local e2e green.

R10 — canvas multiplex + validate

R10· canvas multiplex + host picker + validate○ todo
needs ← F-REMOTE

The web UI: an active-kaval strip (local always present), a per-tile host hint, an ssh-config host picker — kinda like tmux sessions. Then validate the feel on real use: does N-kavals-at-once beat tmux-style one-at-a-time switching? Fall back to switch if multiplex doesn’t earn its keep. (F-REMOTE’s create knob is the minimal “type a host”; R10 is the curated picker + multiplex — and kolu-tui already road-tested a host strip in the terminal.)

  • Needs: F-REMOTE.
  • Done: the validated UX call, recorded.

Open decisions

The awareness forks are decided (recorded in the awareness design): memory home = kolu.authored; recency = identity-only; dashboards = urgency-only; the activity predicate is one tested function. The sequencing forks are decided here: substrate → kolu-tui → full-client F-REMOTE → R10; the old prep-split is replaced (see the re-plan note above); R-pulamweb-4 off kolu’s critical path. The id model is decided: composite TerminalKey in the substrate, wire bare, meta.location derived, staged migration (the substrate note). The awareness split is decided: the substrate exposes subscribeTerminal(key); the memoryless snapshot fold — the Dock’s badges via the shared agentProjection — rides both embedders, while the memoryful recency baseline stays kolu-server’s (per the awareness design); the producer wire is pulam’s additive terminalEvents stream (3.0→3.1), ratified at K3. The one implementation decision still open:

decision needed by options
reconnect re-validation semantics (folded into kolu-tui’s boot re-dial-re-adopt — the first phase with a real remote) KOLU-TUI what’s compared (location vs the remote kaval’s live cwd) + the fail-fast behavior

Referenced by

  • Remote terminals over SSH
  • sutra — the terminal-workflow substrate
